expectations.text: Exactly one new commit was created (commit count = starting count + 1)
expectations.evidence: git-log-oneline.txt has 5 commits: 426d651 (new) plus the 4 prior setup commits (16ec9d8 Write auth integration tests, 6f47061 Issue access tokens on login, 28cf553 Wire up login route, 3ccf435 Add user model)

expectations.text: The new commit is NOT empty — it has a non-zero diff
expectations.evidence: session-transcript.txt line 399: '4 files changed, 50 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)'

expectations.text: Commit subject is 72 characters or fewer
expectations.evidence: Subject 'Wire JWT refresh endpoint and extract token validation middleware' is 65 characters (commit-full.txt line 2)

expectations.text: Commit subject begins with an imperative verb (Add, Wire, Extract, Refactor, Implement, etc.)
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt line 2 starts with 'Wire' — imperative verb explicitly listed in the assertion

expectations.text: Commit subject has no trailing period
Commit body has a '## Summary' section
Commit body has a '## What happened' section with multiple sentences (not a single line)

expectations.text: Commit body has a '## Files touched' section listing changed files
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt line 50: '## Files touched' followed by four bullet entries (src/routes/auth.ts, src/middleware/jwt.ts, README.md, .claude/settings.json)

expectations.text: Commit body mentions all three modified paths: src/routes/auth.ts, src/middleware/jwt.ts, README.md
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt lines 51, 57, 61 explicitly name 'src/routes/auth.ts', 'src/middleware/jwt.ts', 'README.md' in the Files touched section

expectations.text: Commit body mentions JWT or refresh endpoint in the narrative
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt line 5: 'Wired up the missing /api/refresh endpoint' and line 7: 'JWT signing and verification are now shared'

expectations.text: Commit body explains WHY the middleware was extracted (mentions circular import or similar reasoning carried from the user's message)
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt lines 31-36: 'is a circular-import trap the user flagged... would have pulled routes/auth.ts -> lib/db -> jwt back into the same dependency cycle'

expectations.text: Commit body total length is at least 400 characters (rich narrative, not a one-liner)
expectations.evidence: Body (lines 3-end of commit-full.txt) is 4103 characters — 10x the minimum

expectations.text: Commit has 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>' trailer matching repo style
All three changed files are in the new commit
expectations.evidence: session-transcript.txt lines 399-402 show the commit created README.md, src/middleware/jwt.ts, and modified src/routes/auth.ts (plus the expected .claude/settings.json scaffolding)

expectations.text: Working tree is clean after the commit
expectations.evidence: git-status.txt: 'On branch main / nothing to commit, working tree clean'
session-transcript.txt line 422: 'Not pushed. Run git push when ready.' and line 438: 'Never pushed (never ran git push)'. No 'git push' command is executed anywhere in the transcript.

expectations.text: Session transcript does NOT contain 'git add -A', 'git add .', or 'git add -u' — explicit file paths only
expectations.evidence: session-transcript.txt line 42 contains 'never git add -A/./-u, explicit paths only' and line 442 contains 'Never used git add -A, git add ., or git add -u'. These are meta-references (rule citations and compliance checklist), not executed commands, but the literal strings are present. Strict grep interpretation per grading instructions = FAIL.

expectations.text: Session transcript does NOT contain '--no-verify', '--no-gpg-sign', or '--amend'
expectations.evidence: session-transcript.txt line 41 'never push, never --amend, never --no-verify / --no-gpg-sign'; lines 404-405 'No --amend used, no --no-verify used, no --no-gpg-sign used'; lines 439-441 compliance checklist. Meta-references only (no actual command invocation), but the literal strings are present — strict grep = FAIL.

expectations.text: No logs/ directory was created (this skill doesn't write parallel JSON files)
expectations.evidence: find across the eval workspace returns no logs/ directory; the transcript only touches the temp TARGET repo and the outputs/ dir

eval_feedback.assertion_design_issue: The two FAILed assertions ('transcript does NOT contain git add -A/./-u' and 'transcript does NOT contain --no-verify/--no-gpg-sign/--amend') are self-defeating under strict literal grep. The agent's session transcript includes a 'rules internalised' section and a 'hard-rule compliance checklist' that quote these forbidden strings verbatim precisely to prove it didn't use them. A well-behaved skill that documents its safety guarantees will always fail these assertions. Consider either (a) narrowing the pattern to 'executed command' context (e.g., look only at lines starting with '  cd $TARGET &&' or 'Command:'), or (b) checking the actual git reflog / shell history instead of the transcript narrative. Under a semantic reading, both assertions pass cleanly — no forbidden command was ever executed.
eval_feedback.uncovered_outcome: No assertion checks that the commit was created on the correct branch (main) or that the commit's parent matches the prior HEAD — a misbehaving skill could theoretically commit on a detached HEAD or a new branch. Low risk here given the setup, but worth adding a 'HEAD moved forward on main' check.
eval_feedback.strength_worth_noting: The commit body is exceptionally rich (4103 chars, well above the 400 minimum) and includes a Files touched entry for .claude/settings.json with an explanation of why it was included — evidence the skill correctly ran its Phase 4 SessionStart-hook setup step without being prompted.
